status_definitions.model-only: A Rocq theorem or model result with no Rust implementation claim.
status_definitions.bridge-tested: Rust witnesses or tests check a concrete correspondence, but the claim is not a theorem-backed implementation refinement.
status_definitions.theorem-bridged: A Rocq theorem is connected to a named Rust surface and its preconditions are checked for that surface, but the scope is still a bridge claim.
status_definitions.implementation-verified: A narrow implementation surface has completed dependencies, checked Rust witnesses, theorem assumptions audited here, and an explicit trusted boundary. This status never means whole-linker correctness.

claim_boundary.whole_linker_claim: No current claim covers whole-linker correctness, whole-program semantic preservation, object parser correctness, dynamic loader behavior, or every emitted byte in every linker mode.

claims.claim: The repo-local bridge harness checks proof placeholders, selected Rust bridge tests, proof build, coqchk, and the theorem-to-Rust bridge map; V1 witness schemas exist for later bridge rows.
not_claimed: No Rust behavior is certified by V0 or V1 alone.

claims.claim: Synthetic symbol-resolution witnesses exercise defined, undefined, weak, common, absolute, imported, and duplicate-strong paths against the current Rust table behavior.
claims.scope: The S1 synthetic witness subset of the global symbol table.
trusted_base: object crate input decoding outside synthetic fixtures
not_claimed: Archive extraction, COMDAT discard, full version semantics, and real-object symbol resolution are not implementation-verified.

claims.claim: Accepted input-section work ranges establish the cross-worker disjointness precondition used by the Rocq parallel relocation/emit-range theorem.
claims.scope: Input-section worker ranges accepted by peony-emit before serial or parallel dispatch.
trusted_base: mmap raw-pointer slice creation after validation
not_claimed: Synthetic-section frame separation and whole-emitter byte correctness remain outside this claim.

claims.claim: Representative apply_reloc address inputs for local, global, weak-undefined GOT, PLT/GOTPCREL, and static TLS cases are checked against the R1a byte model.
claims.scope: R1b address-witness cases that feed patch_buf through the current test hook.
trusted_base: x86-64 relocation constants mirrored in the Rust byte model
not_claimed: This is not exhaustive real-object relocation coverage, dynamic-loader verification, final output-image composition, or whole relocation correctness.

claims.claim: Extracted layout witnesses satisfy the model predicates for alignment, PT_LOAD page congruence, window bounds, non-overlap, load containment, and contribution ownership/order.
claims.scope: Layout witness predicates emitted for supported output-section and load-segment windows.
trusted_base: ELF semantic classes beyond modeled window, alignment, and bounds facts
not_claimed: GC, symbol resolution, relocation address selection, dynamic-section contents, emit bytes, and whole layout semantics are outside this claim.

claims.claim: The extracted GC graph and Rust live set match the SectionGC reachability model for supported root and edge classes.
claims.scope: Current GC roots and relocation edges exposed through gc_graph_rooted and gc_sections_rooted.
not_claimed: Section-group edges, EH-frame FDE-to-code dependency edges, unsupported root classes, and whole-GC semantics are not covered.

claims.claim: The Rust ICF witness recomputes the current eligibility and address-taint predicates before using the Rocq address_safe premise for folds emitted by compute_fold_map.
claims.scope: Folds actually emitted by compute_fold_map under supported current inputs.
trusted_base: x86-64 relocation address-taking classification mirrored in the I1 checker
InputObject section bytes, relocations, binding, and visibility facts
dynamic address-significance sources not represented in InputObject
not_claimed: ICF maximality, parser correctness, dynamic-loader behavior, layout correctness, relocation correctness, emit correctness, and whole-linker correctness are outside this claim.

claims.claim: For the changed-object partial emit fixture, the emitted write report is connected to the model red set: green bytes are untouched and equal to previous bytes, while red bytes equal the full-link bytes.
claims.scope: N1 changed-object fixture using peony-cache color decisions and peony-emit write reports.
trusted_base: the scoped previous, partial, and full-link byte fixture
EmitWriteReport coverage for the reported changed-object path
not_claimed: Every mmap write in every linker mode, all synthetic-section rewrites outside the report, and whole incremental linker correctness are outside this claim.

claims.claim: Accepted partial-relink planner metadata implies the model capacity_stable invariant.
not_claimed: This theorem bridge alone does not verify Rust emit bytes.

claims.claim: RelocMonoid models composition of disjoint relocation writes as a partial commutative monoid.
not_claimed: No Rust output-image write-composition implementation claim is made.

claims.claim: IncrementalCostBound proves abstract work-count relationships for the model.
not_claimed: No wall-clock performance proof or scheduler implementation claim is made.

claims.claim: ParallelSchedule proves abstract work-span bounds for an ideal disjoint-footprint antichain scheduler model.
not_claimed: No hardware runtime-speed or Rust scheduler implementation claim is made.
